hard.next_step: 6/22 - Ritesh shared a clear timeline for next steps. The ERP implementation is expected to be completed by end of July, followed by a mid August supply chain workshop to identify needs, priorities, and process improvement areas. Based on the workshop outcomes, the project will move forward for evaluation. Prashant: Send out calendar invite for a demo in July.
hard.ais_why:
* Nordic Naturals is digitizing procurement on the back of a major ERP transformation ("SAP S/4HANA and Sage"), focused on CLM, supplier management and spend analysis to align with the new ERP.
* The driver is ERP-aligned modernization rather than AI; the evaluator (Ritesh) is scoping CLM/SRM/spend, with eProcurement and eInvoicing as likely Phase 2.
* Timing slipped with the postponed ERP and budget is unestablished ("Budget - No"); next step is a July demo. Merlin is in scope but not yet a focus. Qualified at 10%.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal is live and paced to the buyer's ERP-gated timeline: Ritesh Gupta gave a clear sequence on 22 Jun (ERP go-live end July, supply-chain workshop mid-Aug, evaluation after), with fresh activity on 01 Jul. The 31 Oct close has a 117-day buffer, but this is an early, unproven Qualified deal with no economic buyer engaged, no champion, an unestablished budget, and a Negative AI-excitement read. It stays On Track only if we run the July demo, multi-thread to Paul Giannini, and use the August workshop to quantify pain and surface the budget owner before commercials.
north_star_verdict.math: 117 days to close (close 2026-10-31, today 2026-07-06). Created 28 May 2026, ~39 days old, in Qualified throughout. No submission-date series fields populated, so time-in-stage equals full deal age (~39 days). LastActivityDate 01 Jul 2026 (event: Clari connect placeholder). Next Step dated 22 Jun 2026 documents a buyer-stated timeline (ERP end Jul, workshop mid-Aug, eval post-workshop). Field history shows no forward close-date slip. At ~39 days in Qualified with a 117-day buffer, pace is ahead of the typical 12-15 month enterprise cycle; the pacing items are buyer-controlled (ERP delivery, workshop), not our execution.
evidence: Next Step dated 22 Jun 2026: Ritesh Gupta shared a clear timeline (ERP completion end July, supply-chain workshop mid-August, evaluation after the workshop).
LastActivityDate 01 Jul 2026: a Clari connect placeholder event is logged, so the thread is live.
Stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified on 29 May 2026; no further stage or close-date movement since.
AIS_Score 3.0 / AIS_Status Negative: the driver is ERP-aligned modernization, not AI; Merlin is in scope but not a focus.
Budget is unestablished per AIS_Why ("Budget - No"); no pricing or commercial conversation has started.
Paul Giannini is tagged Decision Maker (Director of Strategic Sourcing and Procurement) but shows no engagement on recent touchpoints.
No Economic Buyer or executive sponsor identified or engaged.
Single-threaded through Ritesh Gupta (Category Manager Ingredients, Evaluator, primary contact).
No competitor named in any Salesforce field, the Next Step, tasks, or events; competitive landscape is unknown.
No Avoma calls discovered for this opportunity, so buyer pain, criteria, and competition are undocumented and await the August workshop.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists. AIS_Why frames a qualitative driver: Nordic Naturals is "digitizing procurement on the back of a major ERP transformation (SAP S/4HANA and Sage), focused on CLM, supplier management and spend analysis to align with the new ERP", but no cost, cycle-time, headcount, or savings figure is attached to it anywhere in Salesforce, and no Avoma call exists to capture the buyer's own numbers. The 22 Jun Next Step is explicit that the mid-August supply-chain workshop will "identify needs, priorities, and process improvement areas", meaning the buyer has not yet articulated measurable impact. Without a quantified value case we cannot anchor pricing to business impact when commercials begin; quantifying pain at the July demo and the August workshop is a ranked move.
sources: SF AIS_Why__c (qualitative driver: ERP-aligned CLM/SRM/spend digitization)
SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026 (workshop to identify needs and priorities, pain discovery still ahead)
No Avoma calls discovered for this opportunity

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is engaged. Paul Giannini (Director of Strategic Sourcing and Procurement) holds the Decision Maker contact role and is the most senior mapped stakeholder, but he is not active on any recent touchpoint and no field names a budget owner. AIS_Why states budget is unestablished ("Budget - No"), and no pricing or commercial thread has opened, so no conversation has yet revealed who controls the money. For a $150k Source-to-Contract purchase tied to an ERP program, the budget owner likely sits at or above Paul (a VP or CFO), but the evidence does not name that person and none may be invented. Identifying and engaging the budget owner during the August workshop is a ranked move.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Paul Giannini (Decision Maker), Ritesh Gupta (Evaluator, primary)
SF AIS_Why__c ("Budget - No", budget unestablished)
SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026 (no executive sponsor or budget authority named)

decision_criteria.narrative: Evaluation criteria are not yet defined. AIS_Why indicates the buyer is scoping "CLM, supplier management and spend analysis to align with the new ERP", with eProcurement and eInvoicing as likely Phase 2, so the shape of the requirement is directionally known but not yet a criteria set. The 22 Jun Next Step makes clear the mid-August supply-chain workshop is where "needs, priorities, and process improvement areas" will be established, i.e. criteria are downstream of that workshop. No RFP, RFI, use-case list, or BRD is on record, and no Avoma call exists to capture criteria in the buyer's words. Extracting the buyer's must-have criteria and ERP-fit requirements at the workshop is a ranked move.
sources: SF AIS_Why__c (CLM/SRM/spend scope; eProcurement and eInvoicing likely Phase 2)
SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026 (workshop defines needs and priorities)
No Avoma calls discovered

decision_process.narrative: The buyer has stated a clear, if buyer-controlled, process sequence: per the 22 Jun Next Step, the ERP implementation completes end of July, a mid-August supply-chain workshop then identifies needs and priorities, and "based on the workshop outcomes, the project will move forward for evaluation." So the near-term process map is ERP go-live, then workshop, then formal evaluation, gated on the ERP program. What is NOT yet known is the approval chain beyond evaluation: no field names required approvals, multiple-approval steps, or a signature authority, and Paul Giannini's role in the final decision versus Ritesh's evaluator role is undocumented. Confirming who signs off after the workshop and how the evaluation is run is the next process question.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026 (ERP end July, workshop mid-August, evaluation after)
SF OpportunityContactRole: Paul Giannini (Decision Maker), Ritesh Gupta (Evaluator)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are entirely unknown, which is expected for a pre-evaluation Qualified deal. No MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal, procurement-channel, or InfoSec detail is on record, no contract-expiration forcing function is populated, and no SI or implementation partner is named. The only paper-adjacent forcing function is the buyer's own ERP go-live, which gates when evaluation and any downstream contracting can begin. Visibility here is not needed yet; the August workshop and any follow-on scoping calls should surface who owns procurement and legal and whether an InfoSec review will be required for a SaaS purchase at this deal size.
sources: SF fields Current_Contract_Expiration__c, Mandate__c not populated
SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026 (no contracting, legal, procurement, or InfoSec mention)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is directionally clear but not owned or quantified. AIS_Why establishes that Nordic Naturals is modernizing procurement alongside an ERP transformation, focused on contract lifecycle management, supplier management, and spend analysis, which implies pain around manual contract handling, fragmented supplier data, and limited spend visibility that a new ERP alone will not solve. However, this is a company-level driver, not a pain a named stakeholder has articulated with consequences, and the 22 Jun Next Step defers pain discovery to the mid-August workshop ("identify needs, priorities, and process improvement areas"). No Avoma call exists to attribute specific pain to Paul Giannini or Ritesh Gupta. Getting a named owner to state the pain and its cost at the demo and workshop is a ranked move.
sources: SF AIS_Why__c (ERP-aligned CLM/SRM/spend modernization)
SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026 (workshop to identify needs and process-improvement areas)
No Avoma calls discovered

champion.narrative: No champion is developed. Ritesh Gupta (Category Manager Ingredients, Evaluator, primary contact) is the active single thread and has been cooperative, sharing a clear buyer timeline on 22 Jun, which is constructive operational engagement. But there is no evidence he is coaching us on internal politics, the approval chain, competitive dynamics, or that he has access to or influence over the budget owner, so he is a helpful evaluator, not yet an advocate. Paul Giannini (Decision Maker) is a contact role but is inactive on recent touchpoints and shows no advocacy. No Avoma calls exist to reveal champion behavior. Cultivating Ritesh into a coach with EB access, and testing whether Paul will sponsor, at the July demo and August workshop is the priority.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Ritesh Gupta (Evaluator, primary, active), Paul Giannini (Decision Maker, inactive)
SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026 (Ritesh shared the timeline; no advocacy signal)
No Avoma calls discovered

competition.narrative: The competitive landscape is unknown. No competitor is named in Competitors__c or any free-text competition field, the 22 Jun Next Step, or any task or event, and no Avoma call exists to reveal whether Nordic Naturals is sole-sourcing to Zycus, running a multi-vendor evaluation, or planning an RFP after the workshop. Because the purchase is ERP-aligned (SAP S/4HANA), SAP Ariba is a plausible incumbent-adjacent contender, and Coupa, Ivalua, GEP, or Jaggaer are common in a $150k Source-to-Contract evaluation, but none of these is supported by evidence and none may be asserted. The mid-August workshop and the July demo are the gates to ask directly who else is being considered and to plant the Zycus Source-to-Contract and ERP-integration wedge.
sources: SF Competitors__c and free-text competition fields not populated
SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026 (no competitive mention)
No Avoma calls discovered

deal_movement.summary: The deal is live and moving on a buyer-controlled cadence. It qualified on 29 May 2026, the owner changed to Steve Ovadje the same day, and the most recent substantive development is Ritesh Gupta's 22 Jun timeline setting an ERP-then-workshop-then-evaluation path. A Clari connect placeholder was logged 01 Jul 2026. No close-date or amount slip has occurred; the pacing is external (ERP go-live and the mid-August workshop).

competitive_position.summary: Competitive position is genuinely unknown, not sole-sourced. No competitor is named in any Salesforce field, the Next Step, tasks, or events, and no Avoma call exists to reveal the field. The threat is as likely to be status-quo inertia and the in-flight ERP program absorbing attention and budget as a named rival: the buyer is mid-ERP transformation, budget is unestablished, and evaluation does not begin until after the mid-August workshop. Because the program is SAP S/4HANA-aligned, SAP Ariba is the most plausible unseen contender, with Coupa, Ivalua, GEP, and Jaggaer common in this segment, but none is evidenced and none is asserted. The July demo and August workshop are the gates to establish whether an evaluation is competitive and to seat the Zycus Source-to-Contract and ERP-integration wedge early.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: The one documented expectation, ERP-aligned procurement digitization, maps directly to the Zycus Source-to-Contract scope in play; the remaining criteria are undefined until the August workshop.

Paul Giannini (Decision Maker) is inactive and shows no sponsorship. Building Ritesh into a coach and testing Paul's sponsorship at the July demo and August workshop is the priority.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS_Status is Negative (score 3.0): the buyer's driver is ERP-aligned modernization, not AI, and Merlin is in scope but explicitly not yet a focus. This is correct positioning, not under-positioning: pushing an agentic-AI narrative now would misread a buyer who is mid-ERP and scoping CLM/SRM/spend, with Merlin best staged for a Phase 2 conversation once the Source-to-Contract foundation and ERP fit are landed.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 3.0 / AIS_Status Negative. AIS_Why is explicit that the driver is ERP-aligned modernization rather than AI, with Merlin in scope but not a focus. No Avoma calls exist to test whether the field is under- or over-rated, so the field read stands: low AI appetite today, with Merlin a Phase 2 opportunity.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative driver only: digitizing procurement alongside a SAP S/4HANA/Sage ERP transformation, focused on CLM, supplier management, and spend analysis (AIS_Why 22 Jun). No cost, savings, or cycle-time figure quantified.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Early, buyer-gated Qualified deal: Nordic Naturals is mid-ERP transformation and will not begin evaluation until after a mid-August workshop, and with no economic buyer engaged, an unestablished budget, and a Negative AI-excitement read, the near-term win depends on multi-threading and quantifying pain rather than commercials.

win_position.text: The deal is early and unproven: it is Qualified, evaluation does not start until after the mid-August workshop, and no economic buyer, champion, or quantified pain is confirmed (Next Step 22 Jun; AIS_Why).
It is genuinely live and buyer-paced: Ritesh Gupta gave a clear sequence on 22 Jun (ERP end July, workshop mid-August, evaluation after) and activity was fresh on 01 Jul.
Budget is unestablished ("Budget - No") and the AI-excitement read is Negative, so the deal is not yet fundable and Merlin is deferred to Phase 2 (AIS_Why 22 Jun).
It is single-threaded through evaluator Ritesh Gupta with Decision Maker Paul Giannini inactive, so we have no coach and no line to the budget owner.

deal_momentum.text: The buyer named concrete dates on 22 Jun (ERP go-live end July, workshop mid-August), a real pull signal even though it is gated on their ERP program.
Momentum is external and slow: the July demo invite is still ours to send and no commercial, security, or pricing topic has entered the conversation.

customer_commitment.text: Ritesh Gupta is cooperative and proactively shared the go-forward timeline on 22 Jun, showing operational commitment to the evaluation path.
Commitment is shallow: budget is unestablished, no economic buyer is engaged, and the relationship rests on one evaluator (single-thread risk).

deal_risk.text: The evaluation is gated on the buyer's ERP go-live, which already slipped once per AIS_Why, so an ERP delay directly threatens the 31 Oct close.
No competitor is visible and no economic buyer is mapped, so we are blind to both the competitive field and where budget approval sits.

deal_risk.evidence: ERP-gated timeline that already slipped, unestablished budget, single-thread, and unknown competition.
